notes: Server 3.41 adds moved/renamed source reconciliation. When files are renamed (Sonarr/Radarr rename) or moved to a new library path (e.g. anime → animexxx), the old queue rows stop erroring as 'File not found' — they're retired to 'skipped' and a re-scan re-adds them under the new name/path. Runs three ways: automatically every ~60s, at the start of every 'Scan (Find New)'/'Scan All', and via the per-library 'Fix Moved/Renamed' button. Mount-guarded (_source_moved requires a surviving ancestor dir) so a brief drive outage never purges the queue — this also fixes a latent bug where the old Refresh blind-deleted an entire library's queue if the mount blipped. Also REVERTED the v3.40 opt-in 10-bit-HEVC flag: an A/B on Euphoria (S1 plays / S2-S3 fail on a TCL QM7) proved it was never a 10-bit issue — S2 is plain 8-bit H.264 and still fails, and the failing files are spec-identical to thousands that play fine, so there is no metadata signature to auto-detect them. Node stays 2.33.
